detaileddescription.para: The KsolveBase pointers hold the solvers for the PoolBase. At least one must be assigned. Field assignments propagate from the pool to whichever is assigned. Field lookups first check the dsolve, then the ksolve. The PoolBase may be managed by the diffusion solver without the involvement of the Stoich class at all. So instead of routing the zombie operations through the Stoich, we have pointers directly into the Dsolve and Ksolve.

memberdef.argsstring: (double time, const PoolBase *m, unsigned int size)
memberdef.name: checkVal

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int size, unsigned int numThreads, unsigned int method)
memberdef.name: testSyncArray

briefdescription.para: Lookup up parent compartment of pool, return '/' if not found.

detaileddescription.para: Return coords of current pool from parent compt.voxel. The entries differ depending on the kind of voxel.

briefdescription.para: Return [x,y,z] of midpoint of current pool from parent compt.voxel.

detaileddescription.para: Functions to examine and change class between Pool and BufPool.
Changing the buffering flag changes the class between Pool and BufPool.

Volume is usually volume, but we also permit areal density This is obtained by looking up the corresponding spatial mesh entry in the parent compartment. If the message isn't set then it defaults to 1.0.

The PoolBase class is the base class for molecular pools. A pool is a set of molecules of a given species, in a uniform chemical context. Note that the same species might be present in other compartments, or be handled by other solvers. PoolBase is the base class for mass-action, single particle and other numerical variants of pools. Note that in this version it only acts as an interface for the solvers.
